Annual Generation by Fuel Type

Energy-Charts public_power undercounts behind-meter / self-consumed generation. Benchmark = EC + BTM correction: gas +35 TWh (industrial CHP, BDEW), solar +30 TWh (rooftop self-consumption, Fraunhofer ISE), biomass +5 TWh (distributed). The model balances total demand, so it sees the full fleet — apples-to-apples.

Monthly Generation Comparison

Monthly bars show raw Energy-Charts (no BTM correction) — useful for seeing seasonal patterns. For annual totals see the chart above.

Dispatch Comparison (TWh)

Note: Energy-Charts public_power undercounts behind-meter / self-consumed generation. Benchmark = EC + BTM correction: gas +35 TWh (industrial CHP, BDEW), solar +30 TWh (rooftop self-consumption, Fraunhofer ISE), biomass +5 TWh (distributed). The model balances total demand, so it sees the full fleet — apples-to-apples.

Price Distribution Histogram

Model Assumptions

Dispatch method: Rolling-horizon MILP unit commitment (48h windows, 24h stride, CBC solver)

Demand: SMARD 2025 hourly load (466 TWh)

RES availability: Solar 95%, Wind onshore 76%, Wind offshore 82% (bundles MaStR Jan-2026 → 2025-avg vintage, maintenance/outages, profile bias). Wind has no behind-meter; solar BTM is on the benchmark side.

Hydro: Run-of-river 62% CF, Reservoir 52% CF (profiles embed resource variability)

Thermal availability: Seasonal outage profiles (ENTSO-E ERAA + VGB). Winter ~74-90%, summer ~47-78% (planned maintenance + ~1.7 GW reserve coal excluded; "other" carrier ×0.30 to match real wholesale-bid scope)

CHP must-run: 45% of heat-constrained CHP, seasonal availability (88% winter → 70% summer)

Benchmark BTM corrections: Energy-Charts public_power undercounts behind-meter / self-consumed generation. Real adjusted = EC plus: gas +17 TWh (industrial CHP), solar +25 TWh (rooftop self-consumption), biomass +3 TWh (distributed).

Coal: MILP-optimized (no pre-commitment), 8h min-up/6h min-down

Pumped storage: 6.5 GW / 40 GWh, 75% round-trip efficiency, SoC-tracked

Imports: 8 GW cap, MCs calibrated to 2025 neighbor prices (50-95 EUR/MWh)

Fossil MC: Efficiency-diversified (TTF=40, coal=13.6, lignite=5, CO2=75 EUR/t)

Market premium: 35% on MILP dual prices (strategic bidding, ancillary services)

Negative prices: Derived from RES surplus magnitude

Clearing prices: LP dual variables of power balance constraint (post MILP binary fix)
